title: quercus phillyreoides
intro: Quercus phillyreoides, also known as the Philippine live oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Fagaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its high levels of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quercus phillyreoides include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Quercus phillyreoides are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the bark and roots in decoctions or infusions.
